qa_pairs.question: Why might the DDT interface experience lag or unresponsiveness when using X11 forwarding?
qa_pairs.answer: The DDT interface might be laggy and frustrating to use with X11 forwarding because X11 is very sensitive to packet latency, especially if you are not on the same campus as the computer cluster.

qa_pairs.question: How can I resolve lag issues with the DDT interface?
qa_pairs.answer: You can resolve lag issues by running DDT under VNC.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find directions to set up a VNC session for DDT?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find directions to set up a VNC session on the 'VNC page'.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for launching a DDT program if the VNC session is on a compute node?
qa_pairs.answer: If your VNC session is on the compute node, you can directly start your program under DDT as described in the standard instructions.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to manually launch a DDT job if the VNC session is on a login node or a graham VDI node?
qa_pairs.answer: From the DDT startup screen, you need to: pick the 'manually launch backend yourself' job start option, enter the appropriate information for your job and press the 'listen' button, and then press the 'help' button to the right of 'waiting for you to start the job...'.

qa_pairs.question: What is an example command for starting a manually launched DDT job?
qa_pairs.answer: An example command is `/cvmfs/restricted.computecanada.ca/easybuild/software/2020/Core/allinea/20.2/bin/forge-client --ddtsessionfile /home/$USER/.allinea/session/gra-vdi3-1 $PROGRAM ...` where `$USER` is your username and `$PROGRAM ...` is the command to start your program.

qa_pairs.question: What is a common reason for experiencing issues with X11 when trying to use DDT?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are experiencing issues with getting X11 to work, it might be due to incorrect permissions on your home directory.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check the current permissions on my home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check the current permissions with the command `ls -ld /home/$USER`.

qa_pairs.question: What should the output of `ls -ld /home/$USER` look like if only the user has access to their home directory?
Which command removes read and execute permissions for group and other users from a home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: The command `chmod go-rx /home/$USER` will remove read and execute permissions for group and other users.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to restore home directory permissions after modifying them for DDT use?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, after you are done using DDT, you can restore permissions to what they were, assuming you recorded them. More information on how to do this can be found on the page [[Sharing_data]].

qa_pairs.question: Are there any recommended external resources for learning more about DDT?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is a video titled "Debugging your code with DDT" (55 minutes) and a short DDT tutorial available.
